column1: 日本___アメリカが協力する。

column6: および slightly more common in documents

column5: Both acceptable; および more common in general writing
Both formal; および in general business
並びに (kanji form) = very formal; および = standard
並びに = dignified listing; および = standard
または = standard alternation; あるいは = more thoughtful
column6: または more common in documents

column5: または = standard; もしくは = formal/legal
または = standard choice; あるいは = speculative

column1: 在宅勤務___オフィス勤務。
column5: または = general choice; もしくは = legal/formal
column6: または neutral, もしくは formal

column5: あるいは means or/perhaps; 別として = besides

column1: あるいは___知っているかも。
column5: Simple alternative = あるいは + subject; 本当に adds emphasis
さらに = moreover; doesn't intensify the adjective itself
しかも + subject = moreover; まで = even
Both work but context matters; 財政的 = financial
Consequence marker; both acceptable with different emphasis

column5: Depends on context - both form valid consequences
ゆえに is formal, so 我々 (formal pronoun) fits better
Both work; 出席率 = attendance rate (common)
つまり needs full clause for explanation
column6: Clause form needed after つまり

column5: Both work; 早急 more formal/emphasizes speed

Both acceptable; 見方 = perspective/viewpoint

column1: 一方で効率が上がり、一方で___が増える。
column5: Both work; 負担 = burden (formal), 手間 = effort (informal)

Both work; 反対 = opposition (direct), 逆 = opposite (indirect)
